description:
## Background

`MarkdownContent` is the shared, safe Markdown renderer used by both Issue descriptions and comments. It currently renders a `mermaid` fenced block as ordinary code. This slice creates the complete safe reading and recovery path for diagrams before adding navigation gestures.

## What to build

Render every safely supported Mermaid code fence as a Beadsmith-themed Diagram in both Issue descriptions and comments. Each successful Diagram exposes **Diagram** and **Source** tabs, with Diagram selected initially. Source always shows the unchanged authored Mermaid notation. If rendering fails, select Source and show the complete Mermaid error in a persistent inline banner outside the tabs.

## Technical Approach

- Add the full Mermaid runtime as a lazy renderer-process dependency; do not use Mermaid Tiny because it omits diagram types.
- Introduce a narrow shared Mermaid renderer facade rather than calling Mermaid directly from Markdown components. It lazily loads Mermaid, initializes one app-owned configuration, assigns unique render IDs, and serializes `render()` calls because Mermaid configuration/rendering is process-global.
- Configure strict Mermaid security; do not call `bindFunctions`, so Mermaid-authored links and callbacks stay disabled.
- Lock theme and security settings against Mermaid source directives using Mermaid's supported secure configuration mechanism where the installed library permits it. Do not add bespoke directive parsing merely to enforce this slice.
- Derive the Mermaid `base` theme from resolved Beadsmith CSS tokens, normalized to Mermaid-supported hex values. Do not pass raw CSS variables or hard-code a separate dark Mermaid palette.
- Extend the existing fenced-code rendering seam only for Mermaid blocks. Preserve inline code, all non-Mermaid code blocks, GFM, raw-HTML suppression, and external-link behavior.

## Considerations

- Mermaid source is untrusted Issue content. The generated SVG must be mounted only through the controlled renderer boundary; errors must be rendered as text, not HTML.
- Rendering is asynchronous. Ignore stale completion after a source change or component unmount.
- Mermaid's browser SVG behavior is not reliably available in jsdom; test the facade/component contract with mocks and retain real desktop proof for the assembled renderer.
- Every Mermaid type the installed full renderer can safely render is eligible. Unsupported or invalid source follows the normal error/source fallback.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A `mermaid` fenced block renders as a Diagram in both Issue descriptions and comments.
- [ ] Successful diagrams default to Diagram and offer an accessible Source tab with unchanged code.
- [ ] An invalid diagram selects Source and displays the complete renderer error in a banner independent of the active tab.
- [ ] Source directives cannot weaken strict security or override the Beadsmith Theme when Mermaid supports locking those settings.
- [ ] Mermaid callbacks and links are not interactive.
- [ ] Non-Mermaid Markdown behavior, including raw-HTML suppression, GFM, code blocks, and link policy, remains unchanged.
- [ ] Multiple diagrams cannot corrupt one another through concurrent Mermaid rendering.

## Test Plan

- Unit-test the renderer facade for lazy initialization, unique IDs, serialized rendering, strict configuration, source/error propagation, and no callback binding.
- Test Markdown fence recognition, both tabs, source preservation, failure fallback, stale-render cancellation, and non-Mermaid code regression with mocked Mermaid output.
- Run existing Markdown and Issue Detail tests, `pnpm test`, `pnpm build`, and Ultracite checks.

comments.text:
## Implementation plan for bsm-wr7.1 — Render safe Mermaid Diagrams in Markdown

### Goal
Render every safely supported `mermaid` fenced block as a Beadsmith-themed Diagram in both Issue descriptions and comments (they share the `MarkdownContent` seam). Each diagram exposes **Diagram** (default) and **Source** tabs. On render failure, select Source and show the complete renderer error in a persistent inline banner outside the tabs. All other Markdown behavior is unchanged.

### Dependency
- Add the full `mermaid` runtime (`^11.16.0`) as a dependency, loaded lazily via dynamic `import("mermaid")` inside the facade so it is not in the initial renderer bundle. (Not Mermaid Tiny — it omits diagram types.)

### 1. Renderer facade — `src/components/mermaid/mermaid-renderer.ts`
A narrow, app-owned boundary; Markdown components never touch Mermaid directly.
- `renderMermaid(source: string): Promise<string>` returns the SVG string or throws the Mermaid error.
- **Lazy init:** dynamic-import Mermaid and `initialize(...)` exactly once, guarded by a module-level promise.
- **App-owned config:** `startOnLoad: false`, `securityLevel: "strict"`, `theme: "base"`, `themeVariables` derived from resolved Beadsmith CSS tokens (`--color-*` read via `getComputedStyle`, normalized to hex; no raw CSS vars, no separate hard-coded dark palette).
- **Lock against source directives:** use Mermaid's `secure` config list to lock `securityLevel`, `theme`, `themeVariables`, etc., where the installed library supports it.
- **Unique IDs:** monotonic counter → unique `render()` id per call.
- **Serialized rendering:** chain `render()` calls through a single promise queue because Mermaid config/render is process-global (prevents concurrent-diagram corruption).
- **No callbacks/links:** never call the `bindFunctions` returned by `render()`, so diagram-authored callbacks/links stay inert.
- Pure `deriveThemeVariables(readToken)` helper extracted for unit testing.

### 2. Diagram component — `src/components/mermaid/MermaidDiagram.tsx`
- Props: `source: string`.
- Async-renders via the facade in an effect; **stale-guard** with a generation ref so a completion after a `source` change or unmount is ignored.
- Accessible **Diagram / Source** tabs (`role="tablist"`/`tab"/`tabpanel"`). Success → Diagram active; failure → Source active.
- **Source tab** always shows the unchanged authored Mermaid notation (`<pre><code>`).
- **Diagram tab** mounts the generated SVG only through a controlled boundary (ref + `innerHTML`); errors are rendered as text, never HTML.
- On failure, a persistent error banner (Beadsmith `danger` styling) renders outside the tab panels showing the complete Mermaid error.

### 3. Markdown seam — `src/components/MarkdownContent.tsx`
- Extend the existing `pre` override only: inspect the fenced-code hast node; if its `<code>` has `language-mermaid`, render `<MermaidDiagram source={rawText} />` (no `<pre>` chrome). Otherwise render the normal styled `<pre>`.
- Inline code, non-Mermaid code blocks, GFM, raw-HTML suppression (`skipHtml`), and the external-link policy are untouched.

### 4. Tests
- **Facade** (`mermaid-renderer.test.ts`, Mermaid mocked): lazy single init, unique ids, serialized render ordering, strict/secure config, theme-from-tokens, error propagation, `bindFunctions` never invoked.
- **Component** (`MermaidDiagram.test.tsx`, facade mocked): both tabs, Diagram default + source preserved, failure → Source + banner independent of active tab, stale-render cancellation on source change/unmount.
- **MarkdownContent** (facade mocked): a `mermaid` fence renders the diagram in description and comment contexts; non-Mermaid code blocks / inline code / raw-HTML suppression regression stays green.
- jsdom can't run real Mermaid SVG, so these use mocks; real assembled-path proof is deferred to the sibling desktop slice.

### 5. Verify
`pnpm --ignore-workspace test`, `pnpm --ignore-workspace build`, and `pnpm dlx ultracite check`.

### Out of scope (later slices)
Pan/zoom navigation (bsm-wr7.2) and real desktop WebDriver acceptance.

title: Render safe Mermaid Diagrams in Markdown
